states.content:
I'll search for report.pdf in your documents folder.

<tool_call>
tool: filesystem_search
path: /home/user/documents
pattern: report.pdf
</tool_call>

I've initiated the search. I'll wait for the results before deciding whether to try again. Once I receive the search results, I'll let you know what was found. If the file isn't found, I can try searching in subdirectories or with broader patterns.

I see that no PDF files were found in the documents folder. Let me try searching in subdirectories with a broader search:

<tool_call>
tool: filesystem_search
path: /home/user/documents
pattern: report.*
</tool_call>

This will search for any file named "report" with any extension, in case it has a different format than PDF.

Let me try searching the parent directory to see if the file might be located elsewhere:

<tool_call>
tool: filesystem_search
path: /home/user
pattern: report.pdf
</tool_call>

This will search your entire user home directory for report.pdf, which should help us locate it if it exists in a different folder.

I notice the tool results keep showing the same message about /home/user/documents even though I've tried different search paths. Let me try a few more searches to locate your file:

<tool_call>
tool: filesystem_search
path: /home/user/Downloads
pattern: report.pdf
</tool_call>

I'm checking the Downloads folder, as files are often saved there. If this doesn't work, I'll try other common locations like Desktop or the root of your home directory.

I'm noticing that all the tool results are returning the same message regardless of the search path I'm using. This suggests there may be an issue with how the search tool is functioning.

Let me try one more search with a different approach:

<tool_call>
tool: filesystem_search
path: /home/user/Desktop
pattern: report.pdf
</tool_call>

However, I should note that based on the consistent results, it appears that either:
1. The file "report.pdf" doesn't exist in your system
2. There may be a technical issue with the search tool that's preventing it from properly searching different locations

If the file truly doesn't exist, I won't be able to find it no matter how many times I search. Could you confirm:
- Are you certain the file exists?
- Do you remember where you might have saved it?
- What was the original filename (in case it's slightly different)?

This information would help me search more effectively.

description: Real LLM loop scenario on whatsapp
